title: 10 Herbal Creams For Bloating
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great relief for bloating. But have you ever tried herbal teas to help ease discomfort? They work by reducing inflammation in the digestive system and relaxing the muscles that can lead to bloating. When you drink herbal tea, the active compounds in the herbs get absorbed into your bloodstream and start working their magic. For example, ginger (Zingiber officinale) has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues. Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) has a carminative effect, which means it can help release trapped gas and ease bloating. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) has anti-inflammatory properties and can help protect the digestive system from irritation. By using herbal teas, you can experience relief from bloating and enjoy a range of benefits, including reduced discomfort, improved digestion, and more energy. This can be especially helpful for people who experience bloating after eating or during their menstrual cycle. By incorporating herbal teas into your routine, you can take control of your digestive health and feel more comfortable in your own skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate bloating. Gingerols also have a natural ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing gas to pass through more easily. Shogaols, on the other hand, have a stimulating effect on digestion, helping to break down food more efficiently and prevent the buildup of gas. By combining these properties, Zingiber officinale creams can help to quickly and effectively alleviate bloating symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in the ginger essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Slowly pour the distilled water into the oil mixture while whisking constantly. Continue whisking until the mixture thickens into a creamy texture.
Pour the Zingiber officinale cream into a clean glass jar and let cool to room temperature before use. Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains a compound called anethole, which is an essential oil. Anethole has a property called carminative action, which helps to release trapped gas in the digestive system and relieve bloating. Foeniculum vulgare creams also contain estragole, a volatile oil with a similar effect to anethole, helping to ease digestive discomfort and reduce swelling. The compounds in Foeniculum vulgare creams, such as limonene and beta-pinene, also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm the digestive tract and reduce bloating. By releasing trapped gas and reducing inflammation, Foeniculum vulgare creams can help to alleviate bloating and provide relief.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel seeds), 1 cup of coconut oil, and a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a pan over low heat. Add the dried fennel seeds and let it simmer for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the jar. Mix well until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Store the Foeniculum vulgare cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use as needed to relieve bloating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can lead to bloating. The creams also contain flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant properties that can help to soothe the digestive system. Glycyrrhizin and flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms that can contribute to bloating. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these compounds make Glycyrrhiza glabra creams a potential remedy for bloating.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in a small saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the glycerin and dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. Stir until the root is fully dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the cooled mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers or a cream dispenser. Let it cool and solidify before use as a bloat-relieving cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to bloating. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin and turmerones, have been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, reducing symptoms of bloating. Additionally, curcumin has been found to have a positive effect on gut bacteria, promoting a balanced gut microbiome, which is essential for proper digestion and elimination. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, Curcuma longa creams can help alleviate bloating symptoms and improve overall digestive health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the turmeric powder to the melted mixture and stir well to combine.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains several bioactive constituents, including cinnamaldehyde, cinnamic acid, and eugenol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bloating. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles of the digestive tract, allowing for easier passage of gas and reducing feelings of fullness. Eugenol has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can contribute to bloating. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth, Cinnamomum verum creams may provide relief from bloating symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of Cinnamomum verum essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the vitamin E oil and Cinnamomum verum essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely.
Apply a small amount of the Cinnamomum verum cream to the affected area as needed to help alleviate bloating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus creams contains active constituents like boldine, isoboldine, and skimmianine, which help with bloating. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive system. The anti-spasmodic properties of boldine also help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, preventing cramps and gas. The antioxidant properties of skimmianine help protect the gut from oxidative stress, which can contribute to bloating. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and protecting the gut, Peumus boldus creams can provide relief from bloating.
remedies.image_alt: Peumus boldus herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Melt the beeswax in the saucepan over low heat, then add the glycerin and stir until combined.
In a separate bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of dried Peumus boldus leaves with 1 cup of distilled water to make a tea.
Strain the tea into the saucepan with the beeswax mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool, then pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate until solidified.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. Rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid are two key bioactive constituents that help alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. These compounds also possess antimicrobial properties, which can help regulate gut bacteria and prevent overgrowth. The antispasmodic properties of Rosmarinus officinalis creams can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, easing discomfort and cramps associated with bloating. By reducing inflammation and regulating gut bacteria, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can help alleviate bloating and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the distilled water and stir until combined.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil and aloe vera gel, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream for bloating is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthone, menthol, and limonene as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. Menthol, in particular, has a cooling effect that can help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps associated with bloating. Menthone, on the other hand, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate bowel movements and reduce feelings of discomfort. By applying Mentha x piperita creams to the abdomen, you can experience relief from bloating and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of witch hazel, and 1 tablespoon of glycerin.
Combine the distilled water, witch hazel, and glycerin in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Mentha x piperita leaves to the saucepan. Remove from heat and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax to the mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Mentha x piperita cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica creams contains bioactive compounds like ferulic acid, a powerful antioxidant, and volatile oils like bornyl acetate and bornel. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bloating. The ferulic acid also acts as a natural diuretic, increasing urine production and helping to flush out excess fluids that can contribute to bloating. Additionally, the volatile oils in Angelica archangelica creams have a relaxing effect on the digestive system, easing cramps and spasms that can occur with bloating. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion, Angelica archangelica creams may provide relief from bloating symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Angelica archangelica root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Angelica archangelica root to the melted mixture and let it infuse for 2 hours, or until the mixture has cooled slightly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the cream into small jars or containers and let it cool before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Elettaria cardamomum creams contains active compounds like limonene, linalool, and beta-pinene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating. The creams also contain cardamom oil, which has carminative properties that help expel gas from the digestive system. The diuretic properties of Elettaria cardamomum can also help reduce water retention, which can alleviate bloating. By reducing inflammation and gas in the digestive system, Elettaria cardamomum creams can provide relief from bloating.
remedies.image_alt: Elettaria cardamomum herbal creams for bloating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Elettaria cardamomum essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once the mixture is melted, remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the Elettaria cardamomum and peppermint essential oils to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out small amounts of the cream and apply as needed for bloating relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent bloating. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, have natural properties that can ease digestion and reduce discomfort. These teas can calm the stomach and promote the movement of food through the digestive system, which may help reduce bloating. Drinking herbal tea regularly could be a simple way to manage bloating.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for bloating daily can be safe if done in moderation. Peppermint, chamomile, and ginger teas are popular choices for digestion. These teas may help ease discomfort, but be cautious of interactions with medications. It's also possible to develop a sensitivity or intolerance to certain herbs.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bloating, but it's not a quick fix. Some people notice a difference after a few days, while others may take a week or two to feel the effects. It's also worth noting that individual results can vary, and it may take longer to see noticeable changes in bloating.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for bloating can be most effective in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea to work its way through your system and help alleviate bloat before it gets worse. Try sipping on a soothing cup of herbal tea after breakfast or a light lunch to see if it helps ease your discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for bloating. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can make medications like antacids and blood thinners less effective. Others, like chamomile and licorice root, can increase the risk of bleeding or interact with other medications. This can lead to changes in how your medications work.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to talk to your body. Some herbal teas for bloating can be safe, but others might not be. Peppermint and ginger teas are often used and seem okay, but it's best to understand what's in the tea. Look for teas that are clear about their ingredients and say they're safe for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for bloating can be a good option for breastfeeding moms. However, it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger are generally considered safe, but it's still crucial to ensure they don't interact with any medications or worsen any health conditions.
title_2: Older Adults and Bloating: 10 Proven Herbal Creams
